title: barringtonia novae-hiberniae subsp. kassamii
intro: Barringtonia novae-hiberniae subsp. kassamii, also known as the Kassam's Barringtonia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Kassam's Barringtonia are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
